title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Northeastern University President Joseph Aoun's compensation exceeded $3 million in FY 2025, making him the highest-paid in Massachusetts.
Aoun's salary increased from $1.27 million in FY21 to $2.7 million in FY22, due to deferred compensation and bonuses.
MIT President Sally Kornbluth is the second highest-paid with $2,151,788 in FY 2025; others earn over $1 million yearly.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot near Broadway and Norfolk Street, Cambridge.
The shooting occurred shortly before 4:30 a.m., and his body was discovered an hour later by a pedestrian.
Cambridge police, Massachusetts State Police, and the DA's office are investigating; tips can be shared anonymously.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from New England Aquarium volunteered in Cape Town to help endangered penguin chicks with SANCCOB.
Major cared for around 100 chicks, performing feedings and providing medical treatments, to aid their survival.
The New England Aquarium joins efforts to protect African penguins, supporting conservation and rehabilitation in South Africa.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras appeals his seven-game suspension after a brawl with the Nationals resulted in ejections.
Contreras emotionally addresses his recent behavior, linking it to personal struggles and the Venezuelan earthquakes.
Both Contreras and Cade Cavalli have apologized for the confrontation, emphasizing mutual respect and moving forward.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez will represent Boston at the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Aroldis Chapman set a career record with most strikeouts among relievers and has a 2.10 ERA this season.
Ranger Suarez returns to Philadelphia for the All-Star Game, having posted a 2.94 ERA over 16 starts this season.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce expressed his disappointment over the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Pierce advised Brown to use feelings of lack of respect as motivation to excel in his career.
Brown has acknowledged feeling disrespected by both the trade and being left off the 2024 Olympic team.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston marked the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with a parade and traditional readings.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular included performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper, and began with a drone show.
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu participated in the celebrations, joining crowds for the parade and Declaration reading.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS (.977) and home runs (7) over the last 30 days.
The third baseman's improved performance follows early season struggles; he began working with a new personal hitting coach mid-season.
Boston Red Sox have team control over Durbin, presenting long-term roster flexibility with his low-salary contract through 2031.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman broke the MLB all-time relief pitcher strikeout record with his 1,364th strikeout against the Angels.
Chapman achieved the record in 847 innings, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm's 1,363 strikeouts achieved in 1,872.1 innings.
The Red Sox celebrated Chapman's achievement with a video montage of his career highlights in the clubhouse.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston named most collision-prone U.S. city; drivers average 3.7 years between crashes, 189% higher risk than average.
Worcester and Springfield, MA, are also among the bottom rankings; Providence, RI, ranks as 194th collision-prone city.
Brownsville, TX, noted as safest city with nearly 15 years between collisions according to Allstate's report on driving trends.
